name: CopyWithMates2
description: Copies one or more components with mates in this assembly.

parameters.name: ComponentsToCopy
parameters.description: Array of components to copy

parameters.name: Repeat
parameters.description: Array of boolean values; each value indicates whether to use the existing mate reference for the corresponding component to copy; if true, copies the existing mate reference; if false, uses the corresponding entry in the NewEntityToMateTo array for the new mate reference

parameters.name: NewEnityToMateTo
parameters.description: Array of new mate references that map to the Repeat array; if an entry in the Repeat array is false, then the corresponding entry in this array is the new entity with which to mate the component to copy

parameters.name: Values
parameters.description: Array of distance or angle values for the mate references; specify distance in meters and angle in radians; valid for distance, angle, and profile center mates only

parameters.name: FlipAlignment
parameters.description: Array of booleans that map to the NewEntityToMateTo array; each value indicates the corresponding mate's alignment; true to flip alignment, false to not

parameters.name: FlipDimension
parameters.description: Array of booleans that map to the Values array; each value indicates the corresponding mate's distance; true for a positive distance dimension, false for a negative distance dimension; valid for distance, angle, and profile center mates only

parameters.name: LockRotation
parameters.description: Array of booleans that map to the NewEntityToMateTo array; true to prevent the components from rotating, false to allow the components to rotate; valid for concentric and profile center mates only

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.description: Array of longs or integers that map to the Values array; each long or integer indicates the number of clicks in the user interface with which to orient the mate; a positive value indicates to orient the mate clockwise, a negative value indicates to orient the mate counterclockwise; valid for profile center mates only

return_value: True if calling this method succeeded, false if it failed

name: GetDistance
description: Gets the minimum or maximum distance between this entity and the given face or edge entity (see Remarks).

parameters.name: BaseEntity
parameters.description: IEntity of another edge or face to get distance from this entity (see Remarks)

parameters.name: MinDistance
parameters.description: True to return minimum distance between entities; false to return maximum distance between entities

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description:
If MinDistance = False,

and BaseEntity is an edge or curve, specify an array of two doubles (Min, Max), where Min and Max make up the U-parameter range of BaseEntity
and BaseEntity is a face, specify an array of four doubles (UMin, VMin, UMax, VMax), where UMin, VMin, UMax, and VMax make up the UV-parameter range of the surface of BaseEntity

If MinDistance = True,

and BaseEntity is an edge or curve, optionally specify an array of two doubles (Min, Max), where Min and Max make up the U-parameter range of BaseEntity
and BaseEntity is a face, optionally specify an array of four doubles (UMin, VMin, UMax, VMax), where UMin, VMin, UMax, and VMax make up the UV-parameter range of the surface of BaseEntity

parameters.name: Position1
parameters.description: Array of doubles; coordinates of this entity that were used to measure the distance

parameters.name: Position2
parameters.description: Array of doubles; coordinates of BaseEntity that were used to measure the distance

parameters.name: Distance
parameters.description: Minimum or maximum distance between entities; if Parameter is not specified, minimum distance

return_value: 0 if success; -1 if failure

return_value: Index of corner to which to apply the corner relief; used by IFeatureManager::AddCornerReliefType

name: AddCornerReliefType
description: Specifies the type of corner relief to apply to the specified corner of the selected sheet metal body.

parameters.name: CornerIndex
parameters.description: Index of corner to which to apply the corner relief; -1 to apply it to the corner last added with IFeatureManager::AddCornerReliefCorner

parameters.name: ReliefType
parameters.description: Type of corner relief as defined by swCornerReliefType_e

parameters.name: Length1
parameters.description: Not used

parameters.name: Length2
parameters.description:
If ReliefType is swCornerReliefType_e...	Then Length2 is the slot...


swCornerSquareRelief

	Length of the corner relief


swCornerObroundRelief

	Length of the corner relief
swCornerCircularRelief	Radius of the corner relief

parameters.name: Length3
parameters.description:
If ReliefType is swCornerReliefType_e...	Then Length3 is...
swCornerObroundRelief	Slot width of the corner relief
swCornerSquareRelief and FilletedCorners = true	Radius of filleted corner

parameters.name: CenterOnBendLines
parameters.description:
True to center the corner relief relative to the bend lines, false to not; valid only if ReliefType is one of the following:

swCornerReliefType_e.swCornerSquareRelief
swCornerReliefType_e.swCornerCircularRelief
swCornerReliefType_e.swCornerObroundRelief

parameters.name: RatioToThickness
parameters.description:
True to use a ratio value to cut the bend area so that the body can be folded, false to not; the ratios for valid relief types are calculated as follows where t = thickness of sheet metal:

If ReliefType is swCornerReliefType_e...	Then ratios are...


swCornerSquareRelief

	Length2/t


swCornerCircularRelief

	Length2/t


swCornerObroundRelief

	

Length2/t and Length3/t

parameters.name: TangentToBend
parameters.description: True to make the corner relief tangent to the inside bend edges, false to not

parameters.name: AddFilletedCorners
parameters.description: True to fillet the corner relief corners, false to not; valid only if ReliefType = swCornerReliefType_e.swCornerSquareRelief

parameters.name: NarrowCorner
parameters.description:
True to use the algorithm for large bend radii to narrow the corner relief in the bend area, false to not; valid only if ReliefType is one of the following:

swCornerReliefType_e.swCornerSquareRelief
swCornerReliefType_e.swCornerCircularRelief
swCornerReliefType_e.swCornerObroundRelief

return_value: True if successful, false if not

return_value: IFeature

name: CreateDefinition
description: Creates a feature data object of the specified type.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description:
Feature name ID as defined in swFeatureNameID_e:

swFmBaseFlange (sheet metal base flange)
swFmBeltAndChain (belt/chain)
swFmBoundingBox (bounding box)
swFmCirPattern (circular pattern)
swFmCornerRelief (sheet metal corner relief)
swFmCurvePattern (curve-driven pattern)
swFmDerivedLPattern (derived-driven pattern)
swFmDimPattern (variable/dimension pattern)
swFmEdgeFlange (sheet metal edge flange)
swFmFillet (constant radius, face, full round fillet/chamfer)
swFmFillPattern (fill pattern)
swFmFormToolInstance (library form tool)
swFmGroundPlane (ground plane)
swFmLibraryFeature (library)
swFmLocalChainPattern (chain component pattern)
swFmLocalCirPattern (circular component pattern)
swFmLocalCurvePattern (curve-driven component pattern)
swFmLocalLPattern (linear component pattern)
swFmLocalSketchPattern (sketch-driven component pattern)
swFmLPattern (linear pattern)
swFmMateController (mate controller)
swFmMirrorComponent (mirror components)
swFmNormalCut (sheet metal normal cut)
swFmRefCurve (projection curve)
swFmRefSurface (surface sweep)
swFmSketchBend (sheet metal sketched bend)
swFmSketchPattern (sketch-driven pattern)
swFmSMGusset (sheet metal gusset)
swFmSolidToSheetMetal (convert solid to sheet metal)
swFmSweep (boss sweep)
swFmSweepCut (cut sweep)
swFmSweepThread (sweep thread)
swFmSweptFlange (sheet metal swept flange)
swFmTabAndSlot (tab and slot)
swFmTablePattern (table pattern)

return_value: thread, sweep, library, library form tool, tab and slot, bounding box, ground plane, mirror components, projection curve, sheet metal normal cut, sheet metal swept flange, sheet metal gusset, sheet metal edge flange, convert solid to sheet metal, simple fillet/chamfer, belt/chain, sheet metal base flange, sheet metal corner relief, sheet metal sketched bend, mate controller, or pattern-specific feature data object (see Remarks); Nothing or null otherwise

name: CreateSaveBodyFeature

name: DraftXpertChange
description: Changes the parameters on the selected drafted faces, regardless of whether the drafted faces were created manually or with DraftXpert, provided that DraftXpert can process them.

parameters.name: Angle
parameters.description: Draft angle in radians

parameters.name: FlipDir
parameters.description: True to flip the direction of the draft (the draft direction is determined by the selected direction, which could be a planar face, a reference plane, an edge, or two vertices; if you refer to the last argument in IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2, then these items have a selection mark of 1; the faces to draft have a selection mark of 2, and the draft edges have a selection mark of 4), false to not

parameters.name: EdgeDraft
parameters.description: True if this is an edge draft and the user selects edges to draft, false if this is a face draft and the user selects faces to draft (SOLIDWORKS automatically selects the faces next to the edge to be drafted)

parameters.name: PropType
parameters.description:
Propagation type:

0 = no propagation

1 = propagate to the next tangent faces that share the base neutral plane; valid for edge draft or neutral plane draft

2 = propagate to all faces that are a neighbor of the neutral plane

3 = propagate to all faces that a neighbor of the neutral plane on all inner loops (for example, all faces of a pocket or a boss)

4 = propagate to all faces that are a neighbor of the neutral plane on the outer loop (for example, the sides of a box with the bottom face as the neutral plane)

parameters.name: IsStepDraft
parameters.description: True if the draft is a step draft, false if not

parameters.name: IsBodyDraft
parameters.description: True if the draft is a body draft, false if not

return_value: Pointer to the modified draft feature

name: DraftXpertRemove
description: Deletes the draft on the selected faces. If all the faces of a draft are selected, then this method deletes the draft feature; if not, then this method edits the draft feature and removes the selected face references from it.
return_value: Pointer to the feature

name: EditDeleteFace
description: Edits a DeleteFace feature.

parameters.name: Refill
parameters.description:
1 refills the face after it is deleted

0 does not

return_value: True if the operation succeeds, false if not

return_value: 1 indicates that the freeze bar did not move to the specified location in the FeatureManager design tree; 0 indicates that the freeze bar did move to the specified location in the FeatureManager design tree

name: FeatureCutThin2
description: Inserts a thin cut extrude feature.

parameters.name: Sd
parameters.description: True for single ended, false for double ended

parameters.name: Flip
parameters.description: Flip side to cut; true to remove material outside of the profile, false to not

parameters.name: Dir
parameters.description: Reverse direction; true if you want Direction1 to be opposite the default direction (see Remarks)

parameters.name: T1
parameters.description: Termination type for first end as defined in swEndConditions_e

parameters.name: T2
parameters.description: Termination type for second end as defined in swEndConditions_e

parameters.name: D1
parameters.description: Depth of extrusion for first end in meters

parameters.name: D2
parameters.description: Depth of extrusion for second end in meters

parameters.name: Dchk1
parameters.description: True allows draft angle in first direction, false does not allow drafting

parameters.name: Dchk2
parameters.description: True allows draft angle in second direction, false does not allow drafting

parameters.name: Ddir1
parameters.description: True for first draft angle to be inward, false to be outward

parameters.name: Ddir2
parameters.description: True for second draft angle to be inward, false to be outward

parameters.name: Dang1
parameters.description: First draft angle

parameters.name: Dang2
parameters.description: Second draft angle

parameters.name: OffsetReverse1
parameters.description: If you chose to offset the first end condition from another face or plane, then true specifies offset in direction away from the sketch, false specifies offset from the face or plane in direction toward the sketch

parameters.name: OffsetReverse2
parameters.description: If you chose to offset the second end condition from another face or plane, then true specifies offset in direction away from the sketch, false specifies offset from the face or plane in direction toward the sketch

parameters.name: TranslateSurface1
parameters.description: When you choose swEndcondOffsetFromSurface as the termination type for the first end, then true specifies that the end of the extrusion is a translation of the reference surface, false specifies to use a true offset

parameters.name: TranslateSurface2
parameters.description: When you choose swEndcondOffsetFromSurface as the termination type for the second end, then true specifies that the end of the extrusion is a translation of the reference surface, false specifies to use a true offset

parameters.name: Thk1
parameters.description: Wall thickness 1 (mid-plane type uses (Thk1)/2 for each direction)

parameters.name: Thk2
parameters.description: Wall thickness 2 (only used when RevThinDir=3 )

parameters.name: EndThk
parameters.description: End cap thickness (only used when CapEnds=1)

parameters.name: RevThinDir
parameters.description:
Thin feature type:

0 = One direction

1 = One direction reverse

2 = Mid-plane

3 = Two direction

parameters.name: CapEnds
parameters.description:
Cap the ends:

0 = no cap

1 = cap (base features only)

parameters.name: AddBends
parameters.description: True to add auto bends (open profile base features only), false to not

parameters.name: BendRad
parameters.description: Fillet radii if AddBends = True

parameters.name: UseFeatScope
parameters.description: True if the feature only affects selected bodies, false if the feature affects all bodies

parameters.name: UseAutoSelect
parameters.description: True to automatically select all bodies and have the feature affect those bodies, false to select the bodies the feature affects (see Remarks)

parameters.name: T0
parameters.description: Start conditions as defined in swStartConditions_e

parameters.name: StartOffset
parameters.description: If T0 is swStartOffset, then specify an offset value

parameters.name: FlipStartOffset
parameters.description: If T0 is swStartOffset, then true to flip direction of cut, false to not

return_value: Feature

name: FeatureCut4
description: Creates a cut extrude feature.

parameters.name: Sd
parameters.description: True for a single-ended cut, false for a doubled-ended cut

parameters.name: Flip
parameters.description: True to remove material outside of the profile of the flip side to cut, false to not

parameters.name: Dir
parameters.description: True for Direction 1 to be opposite of the default direction (see Remarks)

parameters.name: T1
parameters.description: Termination type for the first end as defined in swEndConditions_e

parameters.name: T2
parameters.description: Termination type for the second end as defined in swEndConditions_e

parameters.name: D1
parameters.description: Depth of extrusion for the first end in meters

parameters.name: D2
parameters.description: Depth of extrusion for the second end in meters

parameters.name: Dchk1
parameters.description: True allows a draft angle in the first direction, false does not allow drafting in the first direction

parameters.name: Dchk2
parameters.description: True allows a draft angle in the second direction, false does not allow drafting in the second direction

parameters.name: Ddir1
parameters.description: True for the first draft angle to be inward, false to be outward; only valid when Dchk1 is true

parameters.name: Ddir2
parameters.description: True for the second draft angle to be inward, false to be outward; only valid when Dchk2 is true

parameters.name: Dang1
parameters.description: Draft angle for the first end; only valid when Dchk1 is true

parameters.name: Dang2
parameters.description: Draft angle for the second end; only valid when Dchk2 is true

parameters.name: OffsetReverse1
parameters.description: If you chose to offset the first end condition from another face or plane, then true specifies offset in direction away from the sketch, false specifies offset from the face or plane in a direction toward the sketch

parameters.name: OffsetReverse2
parameters.description: If you chose to offset the second end condition from another face or plane, then true specifies offset in direction away from the sketch, false specifies offset from the face or plane in a direction toward the sketch

parameters.name: TranslateSurface1
parameters.description: When you choose swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface as the termination type for the first end, then true specifies that the end of the extrusion is a translation of the reference surface, false specifies to use a true offset

parameters.name: TranslateSurface2
parameters.description: When you choose swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface as the termination type for the second end, then true specifies that the end of the extrusion is a translation of the reference surface, false specifies to use a true offset

parameters.name: NormalCut
parameters.description: True to create the cut normal to the sheet metal thickness, false to not (only valid for sheet metal parts; use false for non-sheet metal parts)

parameters.name: UseFeatScope
parameters.description: True if the feature only affects selected bodies or components, false if the feature affects all bodies or components

parameters.name: UseAutoSelect
parameters.description: True to automatically select all bodies or components and have the feature affect those bodies or components, false to only select the bodies or components the feature affects (see Remarks)

parameters.name: AssemblyFeatureScope
parameters.description: True if the assembly feature only affects selected components in the assembly, false if the assembly feature affects all components in the assembly

parameters.name: AutoSelectComponents
parameters.description: True to automatically select all affected components, false to use only the selected components

parameters.name: PropagateFeatureToParts
parameters.description: True to propagate the assembly feature to the components in the model that it affects, false to not

parameters.name: T0
parameters.description: Start conditions as defined in swStartConditions_e

parameters.name: StartOffset
parameters.description: If T0 is swStartConditions_e.swStartOffset, then specify an offset value

parameters.name: FlipStartOffset
parameters.description: If T0 is swStartConditions_e.swStartOffset, then true to flip the direction of cut, false to not

parameters.name: OptimizeGeometry
parameters.description: True to optimize the normal cut in a sheet metal part, false to not; only valid for sheet metal parts and when NormalCut is true

return_value: Cut extrude feature

description: Not implemented.

name: FeatureFillet3
description: Creates a fillet feature for selected edges and control point references.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: Feature fillet options as defined in swFeatureFilletOptions_e (see Remarks)

parameters.name: R1
parameters.description:
Uniform radius of the symmetric fillet; valid only if:

Ftyp != swFeatureFilletType_e.swFeatureFilletType_VariableRadius
Options include swFeatureFilletOptions_e.swFeatureFilletUniformRadius

- or -

Distance 1 radius of the asymmetric fillet; valid only if:

Options include swFeatureFilletOptions_e.swFeatureFilletAsymmetric

parameters.name: R2
parameters.description:
Distance 2 radius of the asymmetric fillet; valid only if:

Ftyp != swFeatureFilletType_e.swFeatureFilletType_VariableRadius
Options include swFeatureFilletOptions_e.swFeatureFilletAsymmetric

parameters.name: Rho
parameters.description:
Value that determines the conic rho or radius of the fillet:

Ftyp != swFeatureFilletType_e.swFeatureFilletType_VariableRadius
Conic rho value [0.05, 0.95], if ConicRhoType = swFeatureFilletProfileType_e.swFeatureFilletConicRho
Conic radius value, if ConicRhoType = swFeatureFilletProfileType_e.swFeatureFilletConicRadius 

(see Remarks)

parameters.name: Ftyp
parameters.description: Type of fillet as defined in swFeatureFilletType_e (see Remarks)

parameters.name: OverflowType
parameters.description: Control of fillet overflowing onto adjacent surfaces as defined in swFilletOverFlowType_e (see Remarks)

parameters.name: ConicRhoType
parameters.description: Fillet cross-section profile as defined in swFeatureFilletProfileType_e; valid only if Options does not include swFeatureFilletOptions_e.swFeatureFilletCurvatureContinuous (see Remarks)

parameters.name: Radii
parameters.description:
Array containing the radii for selected edges for the symmetric variable radius fillet; valid only if:

Ftyp = swFeatureFilletType_e.swFeatureFilletType_VariableRadius

- or -

Array containing the Distance 1 radii for selected edges for the asymmetric variable radius fillet; valid only if:

Ftyp = swFeatureFilletType_e.swFeatureFilletType_VariableRadius 
Options include swFeatureFilletOptions_e.swFeatureFilletAsymmetric

(see Remarks)

parameters.name: Dist2Arr
parameters.description:
Array containing the Distance 2 radii for selected edges for the asymmetric variable radius fillet; valid only if:

Ftyp = swFeatureFilletType_e.swFeatureFilletType_VariableRadius 
Options include swFeatureFilletOptions_e.swFeatureFilletAsymmetric

(see Remarks)

parameters.name: RhoArr
parameters.description:
Array of conic rho or radius values for the specified ConicRhoType for selected edges for the variable radius fillet; valid only if:

Conic rho value [0.05, 0.95], if ConicRhoType = swFeatureFilletProfileType_e.swFeatureFilletConicRho
Conic radius value, if ConicRhoType = swFeatureFilletProfileType_e.swFeatureFilletConicRadius 
Ftyp = swFeatureFilletType_e.swFeatureFilletType_VariableRadius

(see Remarks)

parameters.name: SetBackDistances
parameters.description: Array assigning setback distances on edges meeting at a selected fillet corner (see Remarks)

parameters.name: PointRadiusArray
parameters.description:
Array containing control point radii along the lengths of the selected edges for the symmetric variable radius fillet; valid only if:

Ftyp = swFeatureFilletType_e.swFeatureFilletType_VariableRadius
Options include swFeatureFilletOptions_e.swFeatureFilletVarRadiusType 

- or -

Array containing Distance 1 control point radii along the lengths of the selected edges for the asymmetric variable radius fillet; valid only if:

Ftyp = swFeatureFilletType_e.swFeatureFilletType_VariableRadius 
Options include swFeatureFilletOptions_e.swFeatureFilletAsymmetric

(see Remarks)

parameters.name: PointDist2Array
parameters.description:
Array containing Distance 2 control point radii along the lengths of the selected edges for the asymmetric variable radius fillet; valid only if:

Ftyp = swFeatureFilletType_e.swFeatureFilletType_VariableRadius 
Options include swFeatureFilletOptions_e.swFeatureFilletAsymmetric

(see Remarks)

parameters.name: PointRhoArray
parameters.description:
Array of conic rho or radius values for the specified ConicRhoType at various control points along the lengths of the selected edges for the variable radius fillet; valid only if:

Conic rho value [0.05, 0.95], if ConicRhoType = swFeatureFilletProfileType_e.swFeatureFilletConicRho
Conic radius value, if ConicRhoType = swFeatureFilletProfileType_e.swFeatureFilletConicRadius 
Ftyp = swFeatureFilletType_e.swFeatureFilletType_VariableRadius

(see Remarks)

return_value: IFeature

name: InsertMoveFace3
description: Moves the selected faces on a solid or surface model.

parameters.name: MoveType
parameters.description: Type of move as defined in swMoveFaceType_e

parameters.name: ReverseDir
parameters.description: True to reverse the direction, false to not

parameters.name: Angle
parameters.description: Angle at which to draft the faces; valid only if MoveType is swMoveFaceType_e.swMoveFaceTypeRotate (see Remarks)

parameters.name: Distance
parameters.description:
Distance to translate or offset the faces; valid only if MoveType is one of the following:

swMoveFaceType_e.swMoveFaceTypeOffset 
swMoveFaceType_e.swMoveFaceTypeTranslate and EndConditionType is swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind (see Remarks)

parameters.name: TranslationParams
parameters.description: Array of three doubles for the delta x, delta y, and delta z direction translation (see Remarks)

parameters.name: RotationParams
parameters.description:
Array of six doubles:

First three doubles are the x, y, and z rotation origin
Last three doubles are the x, y, and z rotation angle

(see Remarks)

parameters.name: EndConditionType
parameters.description:
End condition as defined in swEndConditions_e; valid only if MoveType is swMoveFaceType_e.swMoveFaceTypeTranslate

Only the following end condition types are valid:

swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind
swEndConditions_e.swEndCondUpToVertex
swEndConditions_e.swEndCondUpToSurface
swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
swEndConditions_e.swEndCondUpToBody

parameters.name: OffsetDistance
parameters.description: Offset from surface; valid only if MoveType is swMoveFaceType_e.swMoveFaceTypeTranslate and EndConditionType is swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface

return_value: IFeature

name: InsertMultiFaceDraft
description: Inserts a multiface draft feature.

parameters.name: Angle
parameters.description: Draft angle in radians

parameters.name: FlipDir
parameters.description:
True to flip the direction of the draft (the draft direction is determined by the
selected direction, which could be a planar face, a reference plane, an edge, or
two vertices; if you refer to the last argument in IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2,
then these items have a selection mark of 1; the faces to draft have a selection
mark of 2, and the draft edges have a selection mark of 4), false to not

parameters.name: EdgeDraft
parameters.description:
True if this is an edge draft and the user selects edges to draft, false if this
is a face draft and the user selects faces to draft (SOLIDWORKS automatically selects
the faces next to the edge to be drafted)

parameters.name: PropType
parameters.description:
Propagation type:

0 = no propagation
1 = propagate to the next tangent faces that share the base neutral plane; valid for edge draft or neutral plane draft
2 = propagate to all faces that are a neighbor of the neutral plane
3 = propagate to all faces that a neighbor of the neutral plane on all inner loops (for example, all faces of a pocket or a boss)
4 = propagate to all faces that are a neighbor of the neutral plane on the outer loop (for example, the sides of a box with the bottom face as the neutral plane)

parameters.name: IsStepDraft
parameters.description: True if the draft is a step draft, false if not

parameters.name: IsBodyDraft
parameters.description: True if the draft is a body draft, false if not

return_value: Pointer to the newly created draft feature

name: InsertProtrusionBlend2
description: Creates a lofted body or boss from the selected profiles, centerline, and guide curves.

parameters.name: Closed
parameters.description: True closes the loft, false leaves the loft open; if true and less than three profiles are selected, then any selected guide curves must be closed curves

parameters.name: KeepTangency
parameters.description: True maintains the tangency as seen in the section curves, false does not; if the section curves are tangent, then you have the option to specify whether the resulting faces are also tangent; when generating tangent surfaces, SOLIDWORKS maintains planar and cylindrical surface shapes if the section curves exhibit these characteristics

parameters.name: ForceNonRational
parameters.description: True obtains smoother surfaces, false does not

parameters.name: TessToleranceFactor
parameters.description: Factor that controls the number of intermediate sections used for loft with centerline; the default value is 1.0; the greater the value, the more intermediate sections are created

parameters.name: StartMatchingType
parameters.description:
Tangency type at the start profile:

0 = none

1 = tangent to the normal of the profile

2 = tangent to a selected vector

3 = tangency to all the adjacent faces sharing an edge with the start profile

4 = tangent to some of the selected faces sharing an edge with the start profile (not available)

parameters.name: EndMatchingType
parameters.description:
Tangency type at the end profile:

0 = none

1 = tangent to the normal of the profile

2 = tangent to a selected vector

3 = tangency to all the adjacent faces sharing an edge with the start profile

4 = tangent to some of the selected faces sharing an edge with the start profile (not available)

parameters.name: StartTangentLength
parameters.description: Start tangent length

parameters.name: EndTangentLength
parameters.description: End tangent length

parameters.name: StartTangentDir
parameters.description: True is one direction, false is the opposite

parameters.name: EndTangentDir
parameters.description: True is one direction, false is the opposite

parameters.name: IsThinBody
parameters.description: True if this feature is a thin body, false if it is not

parameters.name: Thickness1
parameters.description: Thickness value for the first direction

parameters.name: Thickness2
parameters.description: Thickness value for the second direction

parameters.name: ThinType
parameters.description:
Thin wall type:

0 = One direction

1 = One direction reverse

2 = Mid-plane

3 = Two direction

parameters.name: Merge
parameters.description: True merges the results in a multibody part, false does not

parameters.name: UseFeatScope
parameters.description: True if the feature only affects selected bodies, false if the feature affects all bodies

parameters.name: UseAutoSelect
parameters.description: True to automatically select all bodies and have the feature affect those bodies, false to select the bodies the feature affects

parameters.name: GuideCurveInfluence
parameters.description: Guide curves influence as defined in swGuideCurveInfluence_e

name: ICreateArc2
description: Creates an arc based on a center point, a start, an end point, and a direction.

parameters.name: XC
parameters.description: X value of the circle center point in meters

parameters.name: YC
parameters.description: Y value of the circle center point in meters

parameters.name: Zc
parameters.description: Z value of the circle center point in meters

parameters.name: Xp1
parameters.description: X value of the start point of the arc in meters

parameters.name: Yp1
parameters.description: Y value of the start point of the arc in meters

parameters.name: Zp1
parameters.description: Z value of the start point of the arc in meters

parameters.name: Xp2
parameters.description: X value of the end point of the arc in meters

parameters.name: Yp2
parameters.description: Y value of the end point of the arc in meters

parameters.name: Zp2
parameters.description: Z value of the end point of the arc in meters

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.description:
+1 : Go from the start point to the end point in a counter-clockwise direction

-1 : Go from the start point to the end point in a clockwise direction

return_value: This method creates a partial arc in the active 2D sketch. If a sketch is not active, then a new sketch is created. You can check for an active sketch using IModelDoc2::GetActiveSketch2 or IModelDoc2::IGetActiveSketch2. For COM applications, the object pointer returned from this method can be used to call any APIs on the ISketchSegment interface. The underlying ISketchArc object can be obtained using QueryInterface on the returned ISketchSegment object. OLE applications can define a new ISketchSegment or ISketchArc object using the returned Dispatch pointer. Visual Basic applications interpret the pointer for you automatically, so you can use the returned object to call ISketchSegment or ISketchArc functions. IModelDoc2::SetAddToDB and IModelDoc2::SetDisplayWhenAdded increase performance during entity creation by adding entities directly to the SOLIDWORKS database. IModelDoc2::SetAddToDB also avoids some of the peculiarities involved with creating entities via the user interface, such as inferencing, automatic relations, and snapping to the grid. Adding entities directly to the database also increases the performance of this API. When you are done creating entities, it is important to call IModelDoc2::SetAddToDB(false), to restore SOLIDWORKS to its normal operating mode. This method also works with IModelDoc2::SetDisplayWhenAdded. If you have called IModelDoc2::SetAddToDB(True), additional performance can be gained by calling IModelDoc2::SetDisplayWhenAdded(false) to disable immediate display of entities as they are added to the database. When you are done creating all of your sketch entities, you must redraw your document window (see IModelDoc2::GraphicsRedraw2) to see the entities that you added. You should also restore the original display settings by calling IModelDoc2::SetDisplayWhenAdded(True).

description: Adds a menu item and image to the SOLIDWORKS user interface.

parameters.name: DocumentType
parameters.description: Document type to which to add the menu item as defined by swDocumentTypes_e

parameters.name: Cookie
parameters.description: Cookie as defined in ISwAddin::ConnectToSW

parameters.name: MenuItem
parameters.description:
Menu item string ( e.g., "menuItem@menuString"); SOLIDWORKS creates menu items only if they do not already exist

NOTES:

Specify the name of the menu for menuString (e.g., File, View, etc.) where you want your menu item to appear. If you do not specify menu string, then the menu item appears on the Tools menu below the Xpress Products menu item.
To add your own menu to which to add this menu item, call ISldWorks::AddMenu and substitute the name of your menu for menuString.
Use the & symbol to include an accelerator key, e.g., MyItem@, adds MyItem to the File menu with an accelerator key. To display the accelerator key, press the Alt key. The accelerator key is underlined.

parameters.name: Position
parameters.description: Position where to add the new menu item; the first item is position 0; if -1 is specified for Position, then the new menu item is added to the bottom of the list; this argument specifies the position of the menu item in relation to its immediate parent menu

parameters.name: MenuCallback
parameters.description: Function to call when this menu item is selected (see Remarks)

parameters.name: MenuEnableMethod
parameters.description:
Optional function that controls the state of the menu item (See Remarks)

If specified:

SOLIDWORKS calls this function before displaying the menu item

display of the menu item is controlled by the return value of MenuEnableMethod

If MenuEnableMethod returns...

	Then SOLIDWORKS...


0

	

Deselects and disables the menu item




1

	

Deselects and enables the menu item; this is the default menu state if no update function is specified




2

	

Selects and disables the menu item




3

	

Selects and enables the menu item

parameters.name: HintString
parameters.description: Text to show in the SOLIDWORKS status bar when the user moves the pointer over this menu item; if you specify HintString, then it must be preceded by a comma

parameters.name: ImageList
parameters.description: Array of strings for the paths for the image files for the menu item (see Remarks)

return_value: SOLIDWORKS runtime command ID if successful or -1 if unsuccessful

parameters.name: CallbackFunction
parameters.description:
Function called when user clicks the shortcut menu item; specify an empty string if MenuItemTypeOption is:

swMenuItemType_e.swMenuItemType_Break
swMenuItemType_e.swMenuItemType_Separator

(see Remarks)

parameters.name: EnableFunction
parameters.description:
Optional function that controls the state of the shortcut menu item

If specified, SOLIDWORKS:

calls this function before displaying the menu
displays the menu item according to the return value of this function

If EnableFunction returns...

	Then SOLIDWORKS...


0

	

Deselects and disables the menu item




1

	

Deselects and enables the menu item; this is the default menu state if EnableFunction is not specified




2

	

Selects and disables the menu item




3

	

Selects and enables the menu item

Specify an empty string if MenuItemTypeOption is:
swMenuItemType_e.swMenuItemType_Break
swMenuItemType_e.swMenuItemType_Separator

(see Remarks)

parameters.name: CustomName
parameters.description: Empty string

parameters.name: HintString
parameters.description: Text to display in the SOLIDWORKS status bar when users move the mouse over this shortcut menu item

parameters.name: BitmapFileName
parameters.description:
Path and filename of bitmap for icons (menu bar or menu item)

Specify an empty string if MenuItemTypeOption is:

swMenuItemType_e.swMenuItemType_Break
swMenuItemType_e.swMenuItemType_Separator

parameters.name: MenuItemTypeOption
parameters.description: Type of menu item as defined in swMenuItemType_e

return_value: True if the shortcut menu item is added, false if not

name: AddMenuPopupItem3
description: Adds a menu item and zero or more submenus to shortcut menus of entities of the specified type in documents of the specified type.

parameters.name: DocumentType
parameters.description: Document type as defined by swDocumentTypes_e

parameters.name: Cookie
parameters.description: Cookie specified as defined in ISwAddin::ConnectToSW

parameters.name: SelectType
parameters.description: Selection type as defined in swSelectType_e

parameters.name: PopupItemName
parameters.description: Description displayed on the shortcut menu (see Remarks)

parameters.name: MenuCallback
parameters.description: Function to be called when the user clicks your menu item (see description in ISldWorks::AddMenuItem4) (see Remarks)

parameters.name: MenuEnableMethod
parameters.description:
Optional function that controls the state of the menu item

If specified:

SOLIDWORKS calls this function before displaying the menu

Display of the menu item is controlled by the return value of MenuEnableMethod

If your method returns...	

Then SOLIDWORKS...


0	

Deselects and disables the menu item.


1	

Deselects and enables the menu item (this is the default menu state if no update function is specified)


2	Selects and disables the menu item
3	

Selects and enables the menu item


4	Hides the menu item

(See Remarks)

parameters.name: HintString
parameters.description: Text to show in the SOLIDWORKS status bar when the user moves their mouse over this menu item; if you specify a HintString, it must be preceded by a comma

parameters.name: CustomNames
parameters.description: Semi-colon separated list of the names of the custom feature types; this argument is applicable only if SelectType is a custom feature type (like swSelATTRIBUTES); in the case of swSelATTRIBUTES, set this field to the name of the attribute definition

return_value: SOLIDWORKS runtime command ID or -1 if the method fails

parameters.name: MenuEnableMethod
parameters.description:
Optional function that controls the state of the menu item

If specified:

SOLIDWORKS calls this function before displaying the menu

Display of the menu item is controlled by the return value of MenuEnableMethod

If MenuEnableMethod returns...	

Then SOLIDWORKS...




0

	

Deselects and disables the menu item




1

	

Deselects and enables the menu item (this is the default menu state if no update function is specified)




2

	Selects and disables the menu item


3

	

Selects and enables the menu item




4

	Hides the menu item

(see Remarks)

parameters.name: MenuEnableMethod
parameters.description:
Optional function that controls the state of the menu item

If specified, SOLIDWORKS:

Calls this function before displaying the menu

Display of the menu item is controlled by the return value of MenuEnableMethod

If your method returns...

	 

Then SOLIDWORKS...




0

	

Deselects and disables the menu item




1

	

Deselects and enables the menu item. This is the default menu state with if no update function is specified




2

	

Selects and disables the menu item




3

	

Selects and enables the menu item
